task: Implement token caching and cost optimization strategy for Google Gemini / Vertex AI

key_learnings: Explicit Google Gemini context caching requires a minimum of 32,768 tokens to be cost-effective and accepted by the caches.create API. Smaller contexts will fail/warn on boot.

Google Explicit Cache Rule: When an explicit context cache is created, systemInstructions and tools must be pre-bound directly inside the cache creation payload. Downstream model generation calls must skip dynamic system messages and .bind_tools() to prevent INVALID_ARGUMENT (400) conflicts.

Local Process Lifecycle Handling: Storing explicit cache IDs standardly in state requires lifecycle tracking (expiresAt timestamp) to handle Google's 1-hour cache TTL. Pre-expiry synchronous lookups can seamlessly trigger background auto-reloads without blocking the model factory.

For small-to-medium base prompts (<32k tokens), Gemini's free and automatic 'Implicit Context Caching' is preferred, requiring a 100% stable, appended-only history layout and ANSI sanitization to maximize cost efficiency.
outcomes.patterns_observed: Dynamically bypassing runtime tool binding and system instruction injections in executionNodes when an explicit Cache ID is resolved provides an extremely robust, crash-free interface for Gemini context caches.
